notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. RCA at deliver/rca.md is the authoritative specification — user-reviewed and APPROVED 2026-05-01 in Phase 2 before roadmap creation. Approved fix shape (RCA §Approved fix items 1-7): introduce `ExitEvent` type on the `Driver` trait surface; spawn a per-allocation watcher task in `ExecDriver::start` that consumes the `Child` via `child.wait().await`; gate operator-stop vs natural-exit classification with an `intentional_stop` flag (Arc<AtomicBool> or Mutex-protected) set BEFORE `stop()` issues SIGTERM; add a worker-owned `exit_observer` subsystem that maps exit events to `AllocState::Terminated`/`Failed { reason }`, writes `AllocStatusRow` with a `Clock`-sourced `LogicalTimestamp` strictly dominating the prior Running row, and submits an `Evaluation` to the broker so the reconciler re-enqueues; wire the observer in `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` with shutdown ordering that drains the observer BEFORE the convergence task; add `SimDriver::inject_exit_after` for DST counterpart; rewrite `crash_recovery.rs` to perform a real SIGKILL on the spawned PID (deleting the synthetic obs-write workaround at lines 112-150 and the `tick_n = crashed_counter` advance at lines 152-159).

Two-step Outside-In TDD. Step 01-01 writes RED regression tests (committed with --no-verify per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds): (Part A) a sim-driven test exercising `SimDriver::inject_exit_after` proving the exit-event abstraction works under simulation, plus an `assert_eventually!`-shaped DST invariant pinning the §K2 obligation 'every running alloc whose driver has exited eventually shows non-Running in obs'; (Part B) `crash_recovery.rs` rewrite that performs a real SIGKILL on the spawned PID, asserts the watcher writes `AllocState::Failed` (NOT `Terminated` — kill-by-signal is a crash classification per RCA §Approved fix item 4), expects reconciler re-enqueue via the broker, and expects a fresh allocation to reach `Running`. Step 01-02 lands the seven-item cohesive fix in a single commit.

RED via compile failure (preferred shape per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits). Step 01-01's tests reference public API surface that does NOT yet exist on current main: `overdrive_core::traits::driver::ExitEvent`, the worker-side exit-channel/watcher accessor (`ExecDriver::exit_receiver()` or equivalent — architect picks the cleanest shape consistent with §18 purity), `overdrive_control_plane::worker::exit_observer` (or wherever the subsystem lands), and `SimDriver::inject_exit_after`. The compile failure IS the RED state — it pins the exact symbols Step 01-02 must publish AND prevents any shadow type from surviving. The crafter MUST NOT introduce parallel test-only stubs to make the imports resolve; the imports are held to fail until 01-02 lands the production surface. Both Step 01-01's Parts A and B fail at compile time against current main.

Test placement: Part A (sim-driven exit-event test + DST invariant) lives in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/exit_observer.rs` (new file under the existing inline `mod integration { … }` entrypoint at `tests/integration.rs`). Both tests are gated `#[cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` (no `target_os = "linux"` gate — `SimDriver` is portable). Part B is the **rewrite** of the existing `crash_recovery.rs` — same path, same `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` + `#[cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` gates, same `AllocCleanup` Drop guard at the top of the test body, but the synthetic obs-write phase (current lines 112-150) is replaced with a real SIGKILL on the spawned PID and the `tick_n = crashed_counter` workaround (current lines 152-159) is deleted. The PID is read from `AllocationHandle::pid` (already populated by `ExecDriver::start`); the SIGKILL is issued via `nix::sys::signal::kill` or `libc::kill`. Per .claude/rules/testing.md the rewrite IS the RED state for the production half — it MUST NOT be deferred to a follow-up step. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the synthetic-write pattern is deleted in this PR.

Lane: Part A is integration-tests-gated (default-lane is unsuitable — the exit-observer subsystem has a real `mpsc::Receiver` consumer task that writes to a real `Arc<dyn ObservationStore>` instance and submits to a real `EvaluationBroker`; even with `SimObservationStore` and `SimDriver` the wiring exercises the full subsystem trait surface, which crosses the integration-tests gate boundary per .claude/rules/testing.md §Integration vs unit gating). Part B is already integration-tests-gated. Both run inside Lima VM on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` and on Linux CI via the LVH harness.

Reconciler purity per .claude/rules/development.md §Reconciler I/O remains intact: the exit-observer subsystem is NOT a reconciler — it is a worker-owned task that owns a write seam to obs and submits Evaluations to the broker. `JobLifecycle::reconcile` continues to read from `actual: &State` (populated from obs) and emit typed Actions; it does not gain knowledge of `ExitEvent` directly. dst-lint will catch any `Instant::now()` / `tokio::net::*` / `rand::random()` smuggled into a `core`-class crate — verify with `cargo xtask dst-lint`. The `LogicalTimestamp` counter sourced from the same `Clock` instance the action-shim uses is critical for LWW dominance (RCA §Risk: 'watcher MUST use the same `Clock` source as `action_shim::timestamp_for(tick)`; otherwise watcher writes lose to stale shim writes').

Concurrency discipline per .claude/rules/development.md §Concurrency & async: the `intentional_stop` flag MUST be either an `Arc<AtomicBool>` (preferred — no lock at all in the watcher's classification path) or a `parking_lot::Mutex<bool>` accessed under a critical section that does not span any `.await`. The watcher reads the flag SYNCHRONOUSLY between `child.wait().await` returning and the `mpsc::Sender::send` of the `ExitEvent`. The `ExecDriver::stop` path sets the flag SYNCHRONOUSLY before issuing SIGTERM; the existing `Mutex<BTreeMap<AllocationId, LiveAllocation>>` is the natural home for the per-alloc state including the flag. The watcher's `JoinHandle` is stored on `LiveAllocation::Running` so `stop()` can detach/drop it; on the `stop()` path, after SIGTERM/SIGKILL completes, the `LiveAllocation` slot transitions to `Terminated` BEFORE the watcher's exit event is processed (per RCA §Risk: 'Watcher must update `LiveAllocation` to `Terminated` BEFORE writing obs so a racing `stop()` finds the terminal state' — the architect's chosen ownership shape must preserve this invariant; a candidate shape is for the watcher to NOT mutate `LiveAllocation` and instead let `stop()`'s synchronous post-kill cleanup do that, with the watcher's only side effect being the `ExitEvent` send).

Decomposition ratio: 2 steps with ~6-7 production files modified in 01-02 (driver.rs trait, ExecDriver impl, new exit_observer.rs, lib.rs wiring, action_shim.rs, SimDriver) → ratio 2/7 = 0.29 (well under 2.5). The two-step shape mirrors the prior `fix-restart-backoff-deadline-not-written` bugfix: Step 01-01 is a pure-test RED scaffold; Step 01-02 is a single cohesive GREEN landing. The seven approved fix items are NOT split across multiple GREEN steps — splitting them would land a half-wired exit observer (e.g. trait surface but no watcher; watcher but no observer subsystem) which fails the §K1 'no half-wired subsystem on main' invariant and would not pass the integration tests as a coherent feature.

Mutation testing scope (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per testing.md §Mutation testing exception, prefixed with `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS because `--features integration-tests` is required to give the new tests visibility): `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` AND `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs`. Kill-rate gate ≥80%. Critical mutations to be killed: (a) classification table mutations (e.g. flipping `intentional_stop` boolean check, swapping `Terminated`/`Failed { reason }` arms) — killed by Part A's parametric assertions over the (exit_code, signalled, intentional_stop) triple; (b) `LogicalTimestamp` counter source — a mutation that uses a fresh `Clock` instance (instead of the shared shim source) would lose LWW; killed by an assertion in Part A that the watcher's row strictly dominates the prior Running row. (c) `Evaluation` submission to broker — a mutation that omits the broker submit would leave the reconciler waiting for the next tick rather than re-enqueueing promptly; killed by Part B's 'reconciler re-enqueues without external trigger' assertion. (d) `intentional_stop` ordering — a mutation that sets the flag AFTER SIGTERM rather than before would race; killed by a deterministic stop-vs-watcher race test in Part A. If any mutation survives, add an explicit case to disambiguate it; do NOT weaken the assertion to satisfy the gate. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome.

macOS Lima discipline per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Running integration tests locally on macOS — Lima VM': every `cargo nextest run --features integration-tests` and every `cargo xtask mutants ... --features integration-tests` invocation runs under `cargo xtask lima run -- <cmd>` on macOS. The `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` half of `crash_recovery.rs` is unreachable on macOS at runtime; the `--no-run` typecheck gate catches compile errors but cannot detect convergence-loop bugs. Both Part A and Part B MUST be exercised in Lima before the GREEN commit lands. CI runs Tier 3 via LVH against the kernel matrix (5.10/5.15/6.1/6.6/current LTS) per the standard pipeline; do not split that out into a special path for this feature.

No new dependencies. The approved fix uses `tokio::sync::mpsc` (already in workspace via tokio), `tokio::task::JoinHandle` (same), `std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool` (std), `parking_lot::Mutex` (already in `overdrive-worker`'s deps via the existing `live: Arc<Mutex<…>>`). If during implementation the architect identifies a missing primitive (e.g. a structured cause-class enum that doesn't already exist in `overdrive-core`), surface it explicitly to the user before adding a workspace dep — Cargo.toml edits are not in the approved fix scope.

Spec-document edits explicitly OUT of scope. The defect is purely an implementation gap — the `Driver` trait already documents `status()` (which has zero callers and is being deprecated/removed in 01-02 if the architect chooses, or left in place as dead-code with `#[allow(dead_code)]`; both are acceptable per §Approved fix item 1's framing of `ExitEvent` as a NEW surface, not a replacement for `status()`). User-stories.md and test-scenarios.md prescribe correct behaviour at the spec layer; the implementation just didn't realise the watcher path. Spec amendments to add an explicit 'crashed alloc reaches non-Running within N ticks' scenario at the spec layer are a separate PR if the team wants belt-and-braces.

reviews.scope: RCA + proposed fix direction (seven-item approved fix: ExitEvent on Driver trait; ExecDriver watcher task; intentional_stop flag; worker exit_observer subsystem; lib.rs wiring with shutdown ordering; SimDriver::inject_exit_after; crash_recovery.rs rewrite). Scope: bug fix + regression tests only.
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause (no worker-owned subsystem observes process exits and writes them as AllocState::Terminated/Failed to the ObservationStore; Driver trait has unused status() surface that admitted the gap but never wired the materialisation path); approved approach (mpsc::Sender<ExitEvent> from per-alloc watcher tasks in ExecDriver, consumed by a worker-owned exit_observer subsystem that writes obs rows with Clock-sourced LWW counters and submits Evaluation to the broker; intentional_stop flag gates operator-stop vs crash classification); approved scope (single cohesive bugfix landing the seven items in one GREEN commit; RED scaffold split into sim-driven Part A and real-process Part B; crash_recovery.rs rewrite is part of the RED, NOT deferred). Phase 2 sign-off occurred 2026-05-01 immediately before this roadmap was authored.

phases.name: Wire ExecDriver exit watcher and worker exit_observer subsystem
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 writes RED regression tests in two parts. Part A: a new integration-tests-gated test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/exit_observer.rs` with sim-driven assertions over the new `Driver`/`ExitEvent` surface — (i) `simulated_crash_writes_failed_to_obs_within_budget`: GIVEN a `SimDriver`-backed alloc that has reached `Running`, WHEN `SimDriver::inject_exit_after(alloc_id, 5_ticks, ExitKind::Crash)` is called and the convergence loop advances 10 ticks, THEN obs reflects `AllocState::Failed { reason }` for that alloc with a `LogicalTimestamp` strictly dominating the prior `Running` row. (ii) `simulated_intentional_stop_writes_terminated_to_obs`: same shape but `ExitKind::CleanExit` after `Driver::stop` is invoked; obs reflects `AllocState::Terminated`, not `Failed`. (iii) `assert_eventually_crashed_alloc_reaches_non_running`: DST-shaped invariant test — for every running alloc whose driver-injected exit has fired, obs eventually shows non-Running within the gossip-propagation budget. (iv) `intentional_stop_flag_serialises_with_natural_exit_race`: deterministic two-tick race test where `Driver::stop` is invoked at the same simulation tick as the injected exit; assert the obs row is `Terminated` (operator-stop wins the classification) regardless of injection ordering. All four reference `overdrive_core::traits::driver::ExitEvent`, the worker-side accessor (architect-chosen — likely `ExecDriver::take_exit_receiver()` or a free function on the worker module), `overdrive_control_plane::worker::exit_observer::spawn` (or wherever the subsystem lands), and `SimDriver::inject_exit_after` — none of which resolve against current main. Part B: rewrite the existing `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs`. The setup phase (lines 1-110, ending at `assert!(first_running, …)`) is preserved; the `AllocCleanup` Drop guard is preserved. The synthetic obs-write phase (current lines 112-150, comments + `obs.write(AllocStatus(crashed))`) is REPLACED with a real SIGKILL on the spawned PID — read `state.driver` for the running alloc's `AllocationHandle::pid` (already plumbed through; non-`None` for `ExecDriver`), then `nix::sys::signal::kill(Pid::from_raw(pid as i32), Signal::SIGKILL)`. The `tick_n = crashed_counter` workaround (current lines 152-159) is DELETED. The Phase-3 convergence-tick loop (lines 161+) is updated to assert: (a) within budget, the watcher writes `AllocState::Failed` to obs (NOT `Terminated` — kill-by-signal without intentional_stop is a crash); (b) the reconciler observes `Failed`, emits `RestartAllocation`, the action shim performs stop+start, a fresh `Running` row appears under the same `alloc_id` (per ADR-0023 `mint_alloc_id` determinism). Both Parts A and B fail at compile time against current main (`unresolved import` on every new symbol). Compile failure IS the RED state; commit with `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds. Step 01-02 lands the seven-item cohesive fix in a single commit: (1) `ExitEvent` type on `Driver` trait surface (likely on `overdrive_core::traits::driver`) with fields `alloc: AllocationId`, exit-classification (architect picks shape — likely `kind: ExitKind` enum with variants `CleanExit`, `Crashed { exit_code: Option<i32>, signal: Option<i32> }`, plus an `intentional_stop: bool` discriminator carried alongside or absorbed into the `kind` enum). (2) `ExecDriver::start` reshape: after `tokio::process::Command::spawn`, transfer `Child` ownership into a `tokio::spawn`'d watcher task that calls `child.wait().await` and emits an `ExitEvent` to the per-driver `mpsc::Sender<ExitEvent>`. `LiveAllocation::Running { child, scope }` becomes `LiveAllocation::Running { scope, intentional_stop: Arc<AtomicBool>, watcher: JoinHandle<()> }` (or equivalent — architect picks). The watcher reads `intentional_stop` synchronously between `wait().await` returning and the channel send; on `true`, classifies as `CleanExit` regardless of exit code; on `false` with a non-zero or signalled exit, classifies as `Crashed`. (3) `ExecDriver::stop`: set `intentional_stop = true` (under the existing `Mutex<BTreeMap<…>>` lock OR via `AtomicBool::store(true, Ordering::SeqCst)`) BEFORE the existing `send_sigterm` helper fires. The existing `DEFAULT_STOP_GRACE` and `send_sigkill_pgrp` helpers are reused as-is. (4) New `crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` module with `pub async fn spawn(...)` that consumes the `mpsc::Receiver<ExitEvent>` and for each event: maps `(kind, intentional_stop)` to `AllocState::Terminated` or `AllocState::Failed { reason }`; constructs an `AllocStatusRow` with `LogicalTimestamp` from the shared `Clock` instance (passed in at construction — same source as `action_shim::timestamp_for`); writes the row via `Arc<dyn ObservationStore>`; submits `Evaluation { reconciler: job_lifecycle, target: TargetResource("job/<id>") }` to the `EvaluationBroker`. (5) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs::run_server_with_obs_and_driver`: spawn the exit-observer task alongside the convergence loop; the shutdown token MUST drain the exit observer task BEFORE the convergence task so final exit events land in obs (otherwise the test harness's drop sequence races with final assertions). (6) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs`: when calling `driver.stop(...)`, no shim-side change is required if `Driver::stop` itself is what sets `intentional_stop` (the trait contract carries the discriminator; the shim is unaware). Confirm during implementation; the RCA §Files affected line `action_shim.rs:357,424` flags this as a candidate site only if the trait contract delegates the flag-set to the shim — architect picks the cleaner placement and updates `action_shim.rs` only if needed. (7) `SimDriver::inject_exit_after(alloc_id, ticks, kind)` in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/driver.rs`: schedule the exit on the `SimClock`-driven internal queue; on tick advance, emit the corresponding `ExitEvent` through the same channel surface as `ExecDriver`. (8) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs` rewrite from Part B transitions from RED to GREEN within this commit. All four Part A tests transition from RED to GREEN. Pre-existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p <touched crates>`, `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck), `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`. Mutation gate ≥80% on exit-classification logic in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/{driver.rs,exit_observer.rs}` and on the `ExitEvent` mapping in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs`.

steps.name: RED regression tests — sim-driven exit-event abstraction + crash_recovery.rs real-SIGKILL rewrite
steps.description: Author RED scaffolds in two parts; commit with `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits. Part A — new file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/exit_observer.rs` registered through the existing inline `mod integration { mod exit_observer; }` block in `tests/integration.rs`, gated `#[cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` (no `target_os` gate — `SimDriver` is portable). Four `#[tokio::test]` functions: (i) `simulated_crash_writes_failed_to_obs_within_budget` — GIVEN a `SimDriver`-backed alloc that the convergence loop has driven to `Running`, WHEN `SimDriver::inject_exit_after(alloc_id, Duration::from_millis(500), ExitKind::Crashed { exit_code: Some(1), signal: None })` is called and the harness advances simulated time by `Duration::from_secs(2)`, THEN `obs.alloc_status_rows()` for that alloc shows `state == AllocState::Failed { reason }` (any `reason` string is acceptable; pin only the variant) AND `updated_at.counter > prior_running_row.updated_at.counter` (strict LWW dominance). (ii) `simulated_intentional_stop_writes_terminated_to_obs` — same setup; WHEN `Driver::stop(handle)` is invoked AND immediately followed by `SimDriver::inject_exit_after(alloc_id, Duration::ZERO, ExitKind::CleanExit)`, THEN obs shows `state == AllocState::Terminated`, NOT `Failed`. This pins the `intentional_stop` discriminator: post-stop natural exit is operator-driven, not a crash. (iii) `crashed_alloc_eventually_reaches_non_running` — DST-shaped `assert_eventually!` invariant test: with the convergence loop running, after `inject_exit_after(.., Crashed { … })` fires, within at most `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING` × `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` + reasonable slack ticks, obs shows the alloc in either `Failed`, `Restarting`, `Pending`, or fresh `Running` — NEVER stuck at the original `Running` row. The test polls `obs.alloc_status_rows()` on a bounded loop (e.g. 60 ticks at 100ms simulated each) and asserts the alloc transitions away from the initial Running row's `updated_at`. (iv) `intentional_stop_flag_serialises_with_natural_exit_race` — deterministic race test: schedule `inject_exit_after(.., Crashed { … })` for tick T AND invoke `Driver::stop(handle)` at the same tick T (using the harness's `SimClock::advance_to(...)` to align). Assert obs shows `Terminated` (operator-stop wins) regardless of which side of the race fires first. Repeat the assertion with the order reversed (kill the alloc, then stop) — assert `Failed` because `intentional_stop` was not yet set when the watcher read it. All four tests reference symbols that do NOT yet resolve against current main: `overdrive_core::traits::driver::ExitEvent`, `overdrive_core::traits::driver::ExitKind`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::driver::SimDriver::inject_exit_after`, the worker-side observer-construction surface (architect picks — likely `overdrive_control_plane::worker::exit_observer::spawn` or a public re-export from `overdrive-control-plane`'s public API). The crafter MUST NOT introduce parallel test-only stubs to make the imports resolve. Verify RED with `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` (compile error: `unresolved import` on every new symbol). Part B — rewrite of `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs`. Keep the existing module attributes (`#![cfg(target_os = "linux")]`), keep all imports through line 41 (`use super::cleanup::AllocCleanup;`), keep the test fn signature `async fn killed_workload_is_restarted_with_fresh_alloc_id()`, keep the setup phase up to and including line 110 (`assert!(first_running, "alloc must reach Running before crash");`), keep the `AllocCleanup` Drop guard. REPLACE lines 112-150 (the synthetic crash row construction and `obs.write(AllocStatus(crashed))` call) with a real SIGKILL on the spawned PID: (a) read the alloc's `AllocationHandle` from `state.driver.status(...)` or a new accessor that exposes the PID — architect picks the cleanest path; the PID is already populated by `ExecDriver::start` per `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs:135-138` (`AllocationHandle { alloc, pid: Option<u32> }`). (b) Issue `nix::sys::signal::kill(nix::unistd::Pid::from_raw(pid as i32), nix::sys::signal::Signal::SIGKILL).expect("sigkill workload")`. If `nix` is not already a workspace dep, fall back to `unsafe { libc::kill(pid as libc::pid_t, libc::SIGKILL) }` (this is the only `unsafe` block introduced and lives ONLY in the test file — production code does not gain `unsafe`). DELETE lines 152-159 (the `tick_n = crashed_counter` advance — the production fix means the watcher writes its row at a counter strictly dominating the prior Running row, so no test-side counter advance is needed). UPDATE the Phase-3 assertion loop (lines 161+) to: (i) wait until `obs.alloc_status_rows()` shows the alloc in `AllocState::Failed { … }` (not `Terminated` — RCA §Approved fix item 4: kill-by-signal without `intentional_stop = true` is a crash classification); (ii) continue driving the convergence loop and assert the alloc eventually shows a fresh `Running` row whose `updated_at.counter` strictly dominates the `Failed` row. The assertion-shape phrase 'killed workload is restarted with fresh alloc_id' (the function name) is preserved by the same observable: same alloc_id, distinct PID at the driver layer, distinct `updated_at` on obs. Verify RED with `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` — same `unresolved import` failures as Part A (the rewrite imports the new exit_observer types) AND a new compile error on the SIGKILL path if `nix` import is not resolved. The compile failure IS the RED state. Commit MUST be made with `git commit --no-verify`; commit body cites .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds AND names Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart. No production-code edits in this step. Commit trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`.
steps.criteria: Two new/modified test files: (a) NEW `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/exit_observer.rs` containing four `#[tokio::test]` functions (`simulated_crash_writes_failed_to_obs_within_budget`, `simulated_intentional_stop_writes_terminated_to_obs`, `crashed_alloc_eventually_reaches_non_running`, `intentional_stop_flag_serialises_with_natural_exit_race`) gated `#[cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]`, registered through the existing `mod integration { … }` block in `tests/integration.rs` (one-line `mod exit_observer;` addition). (b) REWRITTEN `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs` — setup phase (lines 1-110) and `AllocCleanup` guard preserved; synthetic-write block (current lines 112-150) replaced with real SIGKILL on the spawned PID; `tick_n = crashed_counter` workaround (current lines 152-159) deleted; Phase-3 assertions updated to expect `AllocState::Failed` from the watcher BEFORE the eventual fresh `Running`. RED verification: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unresolved import` on every new symbol (`ExitEvent`, `ExitKind`, `SimDriver::inject_exit_after`, the worker-side observer accessor). The compile failure IS the RED state. No production-code paths touched: `git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the two test files modified plus the one-line addition to `tests/integration.rs` for module registration. No new modules, no new feature flags, no new files outside the test tree. Commit message: `test(exit-observer): pin Driver exit-event abstraction + crash_recovery rewrite (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit uses `git commit --no-verify` and the body explicitly cites .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Symptom — Part B's rewritten `crash_recovery.rs` is the artefact that demonstrates the user-observable bug: against current main (after the RED scaffolds of 01-01 land but BEFORE 01-02's GREEN), the test fails because no watcher writes `Failed` to obs after the SIGKILL; the alloc stays at the original `Running` row forever; the convergence loop never re-emits `RestartAllocation`. The synthetic obs-write workaround was masking exactly this gap (RCA §Contributing factors).

RCA §5 Whys WHY 5 (root cause: 'no worker-owned subsystem observes process exits and writes them as AllocState::Terminated/Failed to the ObservationStore') — Part A's four sim-driven tests directly assert the missing observation seam: `inject_exit_after` fires → exit_observer subsystem writes obs row → reconciler observes the change. The test (i) `simulated_crash_writes_failed_to_obs_within_budget` is the load-bearing single-cycle assertion that 01-02's GREEN must satisfy.
RCA §Approved fix item 1 (new `ExitEvent` type on `Driver` trait) — pinned by every Part A test's reference to `overdrive_core::traits::driver::ExitEvent` and `ExitKind`.

RCA §Approved fix item 3 (`intentional_stop` discriminator gates classification) — pinned by Part A test (ii) `simulated_intentional_stop_writes_terminated_to_obs` (operator-stop → Terminated, not Failed) AND test (iv) `intentional_stop_flag_serialises_with_natural_exit_race` (the race test verifies the flag is set BEFORE SIGTERM, not after).

RCA §Approved fix item 4 (worker `exit_observer` subsystem maps `(kind, intentional_stop)` to AllocState, writes `AllocStatusRow` with Clock-sourced `LogicalTimestamp`, submits `Evaluation` to broker) — pinned by Part A test (i)'s `updated_at.counter > prior_running_row.updated_at.counter` LWW assertion AND test (iii)'s `assert_eventually!`-shaped invariant that the reconciler eventually observes the change (which it cannot do without the broker submission).
RCA §Approved fix item 6 (`SimDriver::inject_exit_after` for DST counterpart) — pinned by every Part A test's reference to `SimDriver::inject_exit_after`.

RCA §Approved fix item 7 (`crash_recovery.rs` rewrite — real SIGKILL replaces synthetic obs-write; remove `tick_n = crashed_counter` workaround) — pinned by Part B's rewrite. Per the user-facing prompt's explicit guardrail, this rewrite IS the RED state for the production half — it is NOT deferred to a follow-up step.

RCA §Risk — the four sub-risks (stop-vs-natural-exit race; double-stop idempotency; LWW counter discipline; DST coverage gap) are each pinned by a specific test: race by Part A test (iv); double-stop by Part A test (ii)'s post-stop natural-exit shape; LWW counter by Part A test (i)'s strict-dominance assertion; DST coverage by Part A test (iii)'s `assert_eventually!`-shaped invariant.

Tests live behind the `integration-tests` feature gate per .claude/rules/testing.md §Integration vs unit gating (the exit-observer subsystem crosses real-channel boundaries even with `SimDriver` and `SimObservationStore` — the wiring exercises the full subsystem trait surface).

RED verified at compile layer: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unresolved import` on every new symbol. The compile failure IS the RED state; the crafter does NOT introduce parallel test-only stubs.

No production-code edits (production fix lands in 01-02). The only non-test file change is the one-line `mod exit_observer;` addition to `tests/integration.rs` (module registration is a test-tree concern).
Commit uses `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds; commit body cites the rule and names Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.

Any other compile error (typo, missing fixture import, unrelated breakage) is a step-author bug — fix and re-verify.

Both Part A and Part B tests reference the new symbols directly; no shadow types, no test-local stubs, no `mod test_stubs { … }` workarounds (verified by `git grep -n 'struct ExitEvent\|enum ExitKind' crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/' returning no matches; the symbols exist only in production once 01-02 lands).

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix items 1, 3, 4, 6, 7 and §Risk; verify the existing `crash_recovery.rs` setup phase (lines 1-110) covers the Phase-1 setup needed for Part B's rewrite (it does — `Job::from_spec`, `state.store.put`, the convergence-tick loop driving to first Running). Confirm `AllocationHandle::pid` is populated by `ExecDriver::start` (verified — `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs:135-138`). Confirm `tests/integration.rs`'s inline `mod integration { … }` block is the registration entrypoint (verified per .claude/rules/testing.md §Integration vs unit gating layout). RED_ACCEPTANCE — author Part A first (it is more deterministic and pins the abstraction; Part B builds on it). For each Part A test, mirror the existing `crash_recovery.rs` setup shape: construct `ReconcilerRuntime` over a `TempDir`, register `noop_heartbeat()` and `job_lifecycle()`, build `LocalIntentStore` + `SimObservationStore::single_peer` + `Arc::new(SimDriver::new())`, construct `AppState`. Submit a `Job::from_spec` with a `DriverInput::Exec` (architect: this works under SimDriver too because SimDriver classifies on the input shape; if not, switch to a SimDriver-native input shape). Drive the convergence loop to first Running. Then invoke `SimDriver::inject_exit_after(...)` and advance the harness's `SimClock` (architect picks the precise advance API — likely the same `start + Duration::from_millis(tick_n.saturating_mul(100))` cadence as `crash_recovery.rs`). Assert on `obs.alloc_status_rows()`. For Part A test (iii), use a polling loop bounded at 60 ticks. For Part A test (iv), explicitly align tick advancement so `Driver::stop` and `inject_exit_after` resolve at the same simulation time; assert deterministically. For Part B's rewrite, copy `crash_recovery.rs` lines 1-110 verbatim, replace 112-150 with the SIGKILL block, delete 152-159, update the Phase-3 loop to assert on `Failed` first then fresh `Running`. RED_UNIT — N/A (no internal helpers introduced). GREEN — SKIPPED in this step. COMMIT — `git commit --no-verify` with body: `RED scaffold per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits. Part A pins the new \`Driver\` exit-event abstraction (\`ExitEvent\`, \`ExitKind\`, \`SimDriver::inject_exit_after\`, worker-side observer subsystem). Part B rewrites \`crash_recovery.rs\` to perform a real SIGKILL on the spawned PID, deleting the synthetic obs-write workaround. Both fail at compile time against current main (\`unresolved import\` on every new symbol). Step 01-02 lands the seven-item GREEN cohesive fix. Step-ID: 01-01`. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. PRECISION CHECK before commit: `git status --short` shows only the three test files (`exit_observer.rs` new, `crash_recovery.rs` modified, `integration.rs` modified by one line). If any `crates/*/src/` path appears, STOP — production-code edits are forbidden in this step.

steps.name: GREEN cohesive fix — ExitEvent + ExecDriver watcher + worker exit_observer + lib.rs wiring + SimDriver injection
steps.description: Apply the seven-item RCA §Approved fix in a single cohesive commit. (1) `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` — define `pub struct ExitEvent { pub alloc: AllocationId, pub kind: ExitKind, pub intentional_stop: bool }` and `pub enum ExitKind { CleanExit, Crashed { exit_code: Option<i32>, signal: Option<i32> } }` adjacent to the existing `AllocationState` enum (lines 140-146). The `Driver` trait gains a watcher accessor — architect picks the exact shape consistent with §18 purity (the worker subsystem owns the `mpsc::Receiver`; reconcilers never see it). Candidate shape: a separate `pub trait ExitWatcher: Send + Sync + 'static { fn take_receiver(&self) -> Option<tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<ExitEvent>>; }` implemented by `ExecDriver` and `SimDriver`, with the worker subsystem accepting `Arc<dyn ExitWatcher>` at construction. Alternative shape: a method on `Driver` itself returning the receiver. Architect picks; both compose with the seven-item fix. The trait surface is documented in rustdoc with `// SAFETY` / `// INVARIANT` notes for the LWW-dominance and `intentional_stop` ordering rules. The unused `Driver::status()` surface (line 159) is left in place with a rustdoc note pointing at `ExitEvent` as the supported observation seam (or removed if the architect chooses; both are acceptable per the RCA's framing of `ExitEvent` as a NEW surface). (2) `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` — reshape `LiveAllocation`. Replace `enum LiveAllocation { Running { child: Child, scope: CgroupPath }, Terminated }` (lines 47-53) with `enum LiveAllocation { Running { scope: CgroupPath, intentional_stop: Arc<AtomicBool>, watcher: tokio::task::JoinHandle<()> }, Terminated }`. The `Child` is no longer held on `LiveAllocation` — it is owned by the per-alloc watcher task. `ExecDriver::start`: after `tokio::process::Command::spawn`, construct `intentional_stop = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false))`, clone for the watcher; `tokio::spawn(async move { let exit_status = child.wait().await; let intentional = intentional_stop_clone.load(Ordering::SeqCst); let kind = if intentional { ExitKind::CleanExit } else { match exit_status { … classify exit_code/signal … } }; let _ = exit_tx.send(ExitEvent { alloc: alloc_id.clone(), kind, intentional_stop: intentional }).await; });`. Store the watcher's `JoinHandle` and `Arc<AtomicBool>` on `LiveAllocation::Running`. The `exit_tx: mpsc::Sender<ExitEvent>` is constructed at `ExecDriver::new` time and exposed via the architect-chosen accessor. `ExecDriver::stop`: load the per-alloc `intentional_stop` from the existing `Mutex<BTreeMap<…>>` and set it to `true` via `AtomicBool::store(true, Ordering::SeqCst)` BEFORE calling `send_sigterm`. The existing `send_sigterm` / `send_sigkill_pgrp` helpers and `DEFAULT_STOP_GRACE` are reused as-is. After SIGTERM/SIGKILL completes, transition the `LiveAllocation` slot to `Terminated` and detach (or `await` then drop) the watcher's `JoinHandle`. The race invariant (RCA §Risk: 'Watcher must update `LiveAllocation` to `Terminated` BEFORE writing obs so a racing `stop()` finds the terminal state') is preserved: the watcher does NOT mutate `LiveAllocation`; only `stop()` and the observer's idempotent `Terminated` write do. Double-stop idempotency on the existing `Terminated` arm (lines 284-288) is preserved. (3) NEW file `crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs`: `pub async fn spawn(rx: mpsc::Receiver<ExitEvent>, obs: Arc<dyn ObservationStore>, broker: Arc<EvaluationBroker>, clock: Arc<dyn Clock>, node_id: NodeId, shutdown: CancellationToken) -> JoinHandle<()>`. The task loop: `while let Some(event) = tokio::select! { e = rx.recv() => e, _ = shutdown.cancelled() => break }`. For each event: map `(event.kind, event.intentional_stop)` to `AllocState::Terminated` (when `intentional_stop` OR `kind == CleanExit`) or `AllocState::Failed { reason: <"exit code N" | "signal NAME">  }`. Construct `LogicalTimestamp` from `clock.now_logical_counter()` (or whatever the action-shim's `timestamp_for(tick)` derives — RCA §Risk: 'watcher MUST use the same `Clock` source as `action_shim::timestamp_for(tick)`'). Construct `AllocStatusRow { alloc_id, job_id, node_id: node_id.clone(), state, updated_at, reason, detail }`. Write via `obs.write(ObservationRow::AllocStatus(row))`. Submit `broker.submit(Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle"), target: TargetResource::for_job(&job_id) })`. (4) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs::run_server_with_obs_and_driver`: spawn the exit-observer task at server startup. Pass the same `Arc<dyn Clock>` instance the action-shim uses — DO NOT construct a new `Clock` for the observer. Pass the same `Arc<EvaluationBroker>` the convergence task uses. Shutdown ordering: when the cancellation token fires, signal the convergence task to drain, await its completion, THEN signal the observer to drain, await its completion. This ensures any in-flight `ExitEvent` (e.g. a watcher exiting just before shutdown) is processed and its row lands in obs before the harness's drop runs. (5) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs` — confirm during implementation whether the `intentional_stop` flag-set is delegated to `Driver::stop` (architect-preferred — keeps the trait contract clean) or requires shim-side intervention at `dispatch_single`'s stop arm (lines around 357 per RCA §Files affected). If the trait contract carries the discriminator, `action_shim.rs` requires NO change beyond plumbing — the existing `driver.stop(&handle)` call sets the flag inside the `ExecDriver::stop` impl. If the architect chooses shim-side flag-set, document the choice in the commit body. (6) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/driver.rs` — add `pub fn inject_exit_after(&self, alloc: AllocationId, after: Duration, kind: ExitKind)` that schedules an `ExitEvent` on the SimClock-driven internal queue. On `SimClock::advance(...)` ticks, drain due exits and emit them through the same `mpsc::Sender<ExitEvent>` surface as `ExecDriver`. The `SimDriver` constructor gains a paired `mpsc::Receiver` accessor matching the architect-chosen shape from item (1). (7) Step 01-01's RED tests transition from compile-failed → GREEN within this commit. The `crash_recovery.rs` rewrite (Part B from 01-01) executes against real `ExecDriver` + real SIGKILL → real watcher → real obs row → reconciler re-enqueue → fresh `Running`. The four Part A sim-driven tests execute against `SimDriver::inject_exit_after`. Pre-existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace`; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-worker -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-sim`; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck); `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` (Linux real-kernel via Lima); `cargo xtask dst`; `cargo xtask dst-lint` (verifies the new `Driver::status` removal — if architect chose to remove it — does not break the dst-lint surface; verifies the watcher's `Instant::now()` is NOT called from any `core`-class path). Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the seven items land in one commit. No deprecation, no shadow types, no two-phase rollout. The synthetic obs-write pattern is GONE from the test tree (the rewrite landed in 01-01). Commit message: `fix(exit-observer): wire ExecDriver exit watcher + worker exit_observer subsystem` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit landing the seven RCA §Approved fix items): (1) `pub struct ExitEvent` + `pub enum ExitKind` defined in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` with rustdoc covering the LWW-dominance and `intentional_stop` ordering invariants. (2) `LiveAllocation::Running` reshaped to drop the `Child` field; `intentional_stop: Arc<AtomicBool>` + `watcher: JoinHandle<()>` added; `ExecDriver::start` spawns the per-alloc watcher task and stores the handle/flag; `ExecDriver::stop` sets `intentional_stop = true` BEFORE issuing SIGTERM. (3) NEW file `crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` with `pub async fn spawn(...) -> JoinHandle<()>` consuming `mpsc::Receiver<ExitEvent>`, mapping to `AllocState`, writing `AllocStatusRow` via `obs.write` with `Clock`-sourced `LogicalTimestamp` strictly dominating prior rows, and submitting `Evaluation` to the `EvaluationBroker`. (4) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs::run_server_with_obs_and_driver` spawns the exit-observer alongside the convergence loop; shutdown ordering drains the observer BEFORE the convergence task. (5) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs` confirmed (no shim-side `intentional_stop` set if the trait contract carries it; document the choice in the commit body either way). (6) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/driver.rs` exposes `SimDriver::inject_exit_after(alloc, after, kind)` driven by `SimClock`, emitting through the same channel surface as `ExecDriver`. (7) Step 01-01's four Part A tests + the rewritten `crash_recovery.rs` flip RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (the `unresolved import` errors close; the runtime assertions pass against real `ExecDriver` + SIGKILL for Part B and real `SimDriver` + injected exit for Part A). Test verification: (a) all four Part A tests pass: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test integration --features integration-tests -E 'test(exit_observer::)'`. (b) Part B passes: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test integration --features integration-tests -E 'test(crash_recovery::)'`. (c) `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-worker -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-sim` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) exits 0; `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on all touched crates. Mutation gate (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per testing.md §Mutation testing exception, prefixed with `cargo xtask lima run --` per macOS Lima discipline): `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` reports kill rate ≥80%; `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` reports kill rate ≥80%. No surviving mutation on: classification table (`(kind, intentional_stop)` → `AllocState`); `intentional_stop` ordering (must be set BEFORE SIGTERM, not after); `LogicalTimestamp` counter source (must come from shared `Clock`, not a fresh instance); `EvaluationBroker::submit` call (must fire on every exit event). Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Single-cut migration: `git grep 'synthetic_crash\|crashed_counter\|tick_n = crashed' crates/' returns no matches; `git grep '#\[deprecated' crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` returns no matches in the diff. Commit message: `fix(exit-observer): wire ExecDriver exit watcher + worker exit_observer subsystem` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Symptom (closure) — crashed workloads are no longer silently stranded at `AllocState::Running`. The watcher writes `Failed { reason }` to obs with a `LogicalTimestamp` strictly dominating the prior `Running` row; the reconciler observes the change via the broker re-enqueue and emits `RestartAllocation`.

RCA §5 Whys WHY 5 (root cause closure) — the worker now owns the observation seam for process exits per the §4 owner-writer model. The exit_observer subsystem is the natural home for the materialisation path the unused `Driver::status()` surface had merely admitted as a gap.
RCA §Approved fix item 1 (ExitEvent + ExitKind on Driver trait surface) — landed in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs`.
RCA §Approved fix item 2 (ExecDriver::start spawns per-alloc watcher; LiveAllocation reshaped) — landed in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs`.

RCA §Approved fix item 3 (ExecDriver::stop sets intentional_stop BEFORE SIGTERM via AtomicBool::store) — landed in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs`. The race invariant ('watcher must find Terminated state before writing obs') is preserved by leaving `LiveAllocation` mutation exclusively to `stop()` and the observer.

RCA §Approved fix item 4 (worker exit_observer subsystem maps (kind, intentional_stop) to AllocState; writes AllocStatusRow with Clock-sourced LogicalTimestamp dominating prior Running row; submits Evaluation to broker) — landed in NEW `crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs`. The `Clock` source matches the action-shim's `timestamp_for(tick)` source per RCA §Risk LWW counter discipline.
RCA §Approved fix item 5 (run_server_with_obs_and_driver wiring with shutdown ordering: drain exit observer BEFORE convergence task) — landed in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`.

RCA §Approved fix item 6 (SimDriver::inject_exit_after for DST counterpart) — landed in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/driver.rs`. Exit events feed the same trait abstraction as ExecDriver — the same harness asserts 'obs reflects Failed within N ticks of injected exit'.

RCA §Approved fix item 7 (crash_recovery.rs rewrite — real SIGKILL replaces synthetic obs-write; tick_n = crashed_counter workaround removed) — completed in 01-01's RED commit; transitions RED → GREEN within this 01-02 commit.

Step 01-01's four Part A tests + Part B's rewritten crash_recovery.rs flip RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (proving the seven-item fix is load-bearing AND the tests pin the invariants).

All pre-existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p <each touched crate>`, `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck), `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` (Linux real-kernel via Lima), `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`.

Reconciler purity preserved per .claude/rules/development.md §Reconciler I/O — `JobLifecycle::reconcile` is unchanged; the new exit_observer is a worker-owned task, NOT a reconciler. The watcher and observer use `Clock` injection (no `Instant::now()`), `tokio::sync::mpsc` (no raw sockets), `tokio::task::spawn` (no thread::spawn). `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0.

Concurrency discipline preserved per .claude/rules/development.md §Concurrency & async — `intentional_stop` is `Arc<AtomicBool>` (no lock held across `.await`); the existing `Mutex<BTreeMap<…>>` is accessed under non-async sections (the watcher reads `intentional_stop` SYNCHRONOUSLY between `child.wait().await` returning and the channel send). No `parking_lot::Mutex` held across `.await`. No `std::fs::*` in any `async fn` body.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: seven items in one commit; no shadow types; no `#[deprecated]` aliases; no two-phase rollout. The synthetic obs-write pattern is gone from the test tree.

Typed errors per .claude/rules/development.md §Errors — exit_observer's failure modes (channel disconnect, broker submit failure, obs write failure) use thiserror enum variants; no new `eyre::Report` on library boundaries; no new `.expect()` outside CLI binaries; no new `unsafe` blocks (the only `unsafe` is in 01-01's test code if `nix` was unavailable — production code gains zero `unsafe`).

Mutation gate met: kill rate ≥80% on both `overdrive-worker` (driver.rs + exit_observer.rs) and `overdrive-core` (traits/driver.rs). The Part A tests' parametric assertions over (kind, intentional_stop) kill the classification-table mutations; the LWW dominance assertion kills the `Clock` source mutation; the broker re-enqueue assertion kills the `Evaluation::submit` deletion mutation; the deterministic race test kills the `intentional_stop` ordering mutation. If any mutation survives, add an explicit test case that disambiguates it — NOT weaken existing assertions.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (acceptance — `integration-tests` feature gate, runs inside Lima VM on macOS / LVH on CI). Mutation testing scope: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` AND `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` per testing.md §Mutation testing.
quality_gates: Workspace compiles green: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` exits 0 on macOS (no integration-tests feature); `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` exits 0 on macOS (typecheck gate).
Default-lane tests green: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-worker -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-sim` exits 0.

Step 01-01's four Part A tests transition RED → GREEN without modification: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test integration --features integration-tests -E 'test(exit_observer::)'` exits 0 with all four passing.

Step 01-01's Part B (`crash_recovery.rs` rewrite) transitions RED → GREEN without modification: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test integration --features integration-tests -E 'test(crash_recovery::)'` exits 0.

Full integration run green inside Lima VM: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0. Surfaces any pre-existing test that the new exit_observer disturbs (e.g. tests that previously relied on the alloc staying at `Running` indefinitely after a non-clean exit; per RCA §Risk these were exercising the bug, not the spec — adjust the fixture, not the assertion).

DST run green: `cargo xtask dst` exits 0. The new `assert_eventually!`-shaped invariant 'every running alloc whose driver-injected exit has fired eventually shows non-Running in obs' is exercised across the seeded scenario set.

dst-lint clean: `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0. Verifies no `Instant::now()` / `tokio::net::*` / `rand::random()` smuggled into any `core`-class crate. The watcher's `Clock` injection is the dst-lint-compliant time source; the broker re-enqueue uses the existing trait surface.

Clippy clean: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0 across all touched crates. Pay particular attention to `clippy::await_holding_lock` (no `tokio::sync::Mutex` held across `.await`; the existing `parking_lot::Mutex<BTreeMap<…>>` is non-async).

Single-cut migration: `git grep 'synthetic_crash\|tick_n = crashed_counter' crates/` returns no matches; `git grep '#\[deprecated' crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/driver.rs` returns no matches in the diff. The synthetic obs-write pattern is gone.

Production edit precision: `git diff HEAD~1 -- crates/` shows exactly the seven files listed in `files_to_modify` (plus the test files from 01-01 already landed in the prior commit). No incidental edits; no unrelated refactors. The `Driver::status()` surface is either left in place with a rustdoc note OR removed — both are acceptable per the RCA's framing; the choice is documented in the commit body.

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix items 1-7 and §Risk; locate current symbols by name (NOT line number — line numbers may have drifted): `ExecDriver::start`, `ExecDriver::stop`, `LiveAllocation`, `Driver` trait, `AllocationState`, `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`, `dispatch_single`, `SimDriver::start`. Use Serena's symbolic tools (`find_symbol`, `replace_symbol_body`, `insert_after_symbol`) for every production edit — the changes are localised to named symbols and are addressable symbolically. Confirm `tokio::sync::mpsc`, `tokio::task::JoinHandle`, `std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool`, `parking_lot::Mutex` are all already in the workspace dep graph (verified — no new deps needed). RED_ACCEPTANCE — Step 01-01 already wrote the outermost RED tests; verify they currently fail at compile time inside Lima (`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unresolved import`). RED_UNIT — N/A (no internal helpers introduced beyond what the seven items prescribe; the exit_observer `spawn` fn is the only new public symbol on the worker side). GREEN — implement in this order, verifying compile-clean after each: (1) Add `ExitEvent` + `ExitKind` to `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs`. Add the architect-chosen accessor (separate `ExitWatcher` trait OR method on `Driver`). Verify `cargo check -p overdrive-core` exits 0. (2) Reshape `LiveAllocation` and `ExecDriver::{start,stop}` in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs`. The watcher closure is the load-bearing change — pay particular attention to the `Ordering::SeqCst` on the AtomicBool load/store (relax to `Acquire`/`Release` only after benchmarking; the current rule is 'use the strongest ordering until profiling demonstrates a hot spot'). The `Child` is moved into the spawned task; `LiveAllocation::Running` no longer holds it. The watcher reads `intentional_stop` synchronously between `wait().await` returning and the channel send — this is the §Concurrency rule that the lock is NOT held across `.await`; verify with `clippy::await_holding_lock`. Verify `cargo check -p overdrive-worker` exits 0. (3) Author NEW file `crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` with `pub async fn spawn(...)`. Re-export from `crates/overdrive-worker/src/lib.rs`. The `LogicalTimestamp` construction uses the same `Clock` instance the action-shim uses — verify by reading `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs::timestamp_for` to confirm the `Clock` source. The `Evaluation` submission uses `EvaluationBroker::submit` — check the existing API for the exact method name. Verify `cargo check -p overdrive-worker` exits 0. (4) Wire `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`. Pass the same `Arc<dyn Clock>` and `Arc<EvaluationBroker>` instances that already exist in `AppState`. Shutdown ordering: extend the existing `CancellationToken` plumbing to drain the observer task before the convergence task. Verify `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0. (5) Confirm `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs` requires no change (the trait contract carries `intentional_stop`); document the choice in the commit body. (6) Add `SimDriver::inject_exit_after` to `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/driver.rs`. The internal queue is driven by `SimClock::advance(...)`; check the existing `SimClock` API for the advance hook. Verify `cargo check -p overdrive-sim` exits 0. (7) Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` — should now compile (Step 01-01's `unresolved import` errors all close). Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test integration --features integration-tests -E 'test(exit_observer::)'` — Step 01-01's four Part A tests pass. Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test integration --features integration-tests -E 'test(crash_recovery::)'` — Part B passes. (8) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace` — full default lane green. (9) Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`. Surface any pre-existing test that now fails (e.g. tests assuming the alloc stays Running indefinitely after a child exit). Per RCA §Risk: adjust the test fixture, NOT the assertion. (10) Run `cargo xtask dst` — green. (11) Run `cargo xtask dst-lint` — green. (12) Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — green. COMMIT — `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); the commit body explicitly enumerates the seven items with file paths AND confirms Step 01-01's tests transition RED → GREEN within this commit AND documents the action_shim.rs choice (no change OR shim-side flag-set). Single cohesive commit. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per testing.md §Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`, prefixed with `cargo xtask lima run --` per macOS Lima discipline. Invoke `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/exit_observer.rs` AND `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs`; wait for completion via the shell ID; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed: (a) `kind == ExitKind::CleanExit` → `kind != ExitKind::CleanExit` (classification flip): killed by Part A test (i)/(ii). (b) `intentional_stop` check inversion: killed by Part A test (ii) (post-stop natural exit must be Terminated, not Failed). (c) `intentional_stop.store(true, ...)` MOVED to AFTER `send_sigterm` (ordering bug): killed by Part A test (iv) (the deterministic race test). (d) Deletion of `obs.write(...)` call: killed by Part A test (i)'s LWW dominance assertion. (e) Deletion of `broker.submit(...)` call: killed by Part A test (iii)'s `assert_eventually!` invariant (without the broker submit, the reconciler waits for the next periodic tick rather than re-enqueueing — beyond the assertion budget). (f) Replacement of shared `Clock` instance with a fresh one (e.g. `Arc::new(SystemClock::new())`): killed by Part A test (i)'s `updated_at.counter > prior_running_row.updated_at.counter` strict-dominance assertion (a fresh Clock starts at counter 0 and loses LWW). On any surviving mutation, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it AND re-run. Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`. Post-commit smoke check: re-run `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-worker -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-sim`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, and `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` one more time to confirm zero regressions across the full workspace; this is the input to Phase 3.5 post-merge integration gate.
